rationale: Shortage is inside two weeks and an open PO can arrive sooner than a new order.
Another DC has transferable surplus and can recover risk faster than supplier replenishment.
Primary supplier reliability dropped below policy threshold so alternate supply wins.
Standard reorder meets service target without expedite premium or transfer dependency.
Inventory is materially above demand horizon so suppress replenishment and consider rebalancing.
No supplier or transfer option can recover service in time so the planner must escalate allocation decisions.
